question: why being good at being a person is like being good at anything else
expected_answer:
We see a lot of talk about how a person can try to be a good person. In this talk a person looks at the way they act, the way they feel, and the way they act toward other people. They watch how they change over time and how other people say what they think. This watching and changing feels a lot like the way a person might try to be good at a game or work. The scene is that many people sit and think about why the work of being a good person seems to follow the same steps as the work of being good at any other thing.

The thing that is happening is that a person takes the thing they want, such as “be nice” or “be just”, and then they look at what they do each day. They get the way other people tell them how they did, from themselves, and from the way the world shows a change. They try again, they learn a little, they try again, and they feel a little better when they see that the world seems a bit nicer. All of this looks a lot like the way a person learns to play a game, to draw, or to fix a thing that works. The hard question that comes up is why the work of being a good person should be thought of as the same as the work of being good at any other thing. Is there something special about the feeling part of being a person, or is it just another set of things they do that can be tried, watched, and changed?

One answer that many people give is that being a good person is just a set of things they do that can be seen and tried. They say that being nice, telling truth, and care are things they do that can be done many times like a game move. When a person watches how those things work, they can do the same as them, they can get a feel for when they work, and they can get better with time. This answer rests on the idea that any thing you get good at, whether it is a game move or a caring act, is learned by trying, by the way other people tell them how they did, and by changing what they do.

Another answer that some people share is that being a good person is not only about things they do but also about feelings and other people. They point out that when you care for someone you feel something inside, and that feeling can push you to act in a certain way. Because feelings are part of the way it goes, they think the work of being a good person may need a different kind of care than the work of fixing a thing you use. They say the need to feel safe, to feel loved, or to feel that you belong can make the learning path a bit different, even if the steps of trying and watching are still there.

A third answer comes from those who look at the ideas that big groups of people make. They say that big groups of people make a set of ideas about what it means to be good, and those ideas act like a game with signs and ideas that tell what to do. In that view, being a good person is like playing a game where you try to follow the ideas, get a good feeling, and avoid a bad feeling. Because a game has a clear set of moves and a clear way to see if you do well, they think the work of being good at a person is very much like the work of being good at any other game.

All of these answers try to tell about why the work of being a good person looks a lot like the work of being good at other things. Some say it is because both are sets of things they do we can try and change. Some say it is because feelings add an extra part that makes it a bit special. Some say it is because the ideas we share act like a game. The talk continues as people think about which of these ideas feels the most true and how they might use that thought to keep trying to be a better person, just as they would keep trying to be better at any other thing they care about.
english_query: why being good at being a person is like being good at anything else

First, we see a person who learns how to act in a kind or fair way and we watch how that person lives. When we look at the person we notice that the same steps that help a person get good at a sport, a game, a job, or a craft are also used to get good at being a kind or fair person. The person has to pay attention, to try again after a miss, to get advice, and to keep a habit of checking what they do. This makes us think about a hard question: is being good at being a person just another kind of skill, the same as any other skill we can learn, or is it something that is not like a skill at all?

Some people answer that it is a skill. They say that a person can practice listening, can practice not getting angry, can practice giving help, and can get better the more they try. They point to the fact that teachers can give lessons, that books can give tips, and that people can see how they improve over time, just like a player who gets better at a game. Because of this, they think that being good at being a person is something we can work on and measure, and that it does not need a special word beyond “skill”.

Other people answer that it is not a skill in the same way. They say that being a good person is tied to what we think is right or wrong, to the way we feel about others, and to the way we want to be seen. They argue that a skill can be judged by how well a task is done, but that a good person is judged by deeper feelings and by the way we treat others, which may not be counted the same way as a score or a win. They also note that some parts of being a good person may come from who we are inside, not just from practice, and that we may need more than just repetition to have that kind of good.

Both sides give reasons that make sense. The first side shows that practice, feedback, and habit can shape the way we act toward others, just as they shape any other thing we try to learn. The second side shows that the feeling of right and wrong and the wish to be kind can feel like something that is more than a simple practice, something that lives in the heart and mind together. The hard question stays in the middle, and each answer helps us think about how we can grow and what we mean when we say we are “good” at being a person.

question: analogy_proper analogy (explanatory) — epistemology
expected_answer:
The idea of picking the most good guess about why something happened is in a place where people do not all agree today: many people use it a lot when they think about how the world works, and many think it is a right way to think about what we know, but the reason why it works in a very hard number way or a way that all parts fit together is still talked about.  

A story taken from a law person thinking helps show the form of this idea about picking a guess with clear look.  

When a good law person builds a story from signs that are not big by them, the way of thinking is one of many signs coming together: many different signs – a foot mark, a money reason, a story that does not fit, a piece that is the same, a picture time mark – none of which alone tells the whole, are each made make sense by one guess about what went on.  

The coming together is big for what we know because the signs are different: if the guess is not true, the chance that all these different signs would happen at the same time is low.  

The more different signs come together, the more sure we can say that the guess that fits them best is right.  

This is the idea of picking the most good guess about why something happened in its clear form, and the law story shows its way by putting it in a known story way.  

The same form is under the way people think about how the world works in cases where many ideas come together.  

The idea that tiny parts make up things was shown not by one try but by many different things coming together – tiny moving parts, a same form of parts, a light line set, how air works when pushed – each of which the idea made make sense of and none of which alone told the whole.  

The idea about how living things change was shown by many signs coming together from old bones, where living things live, looking at body parts side by side, and tries of how people raise animals.  

In each story, the power of the idea comes from the many and different things it puts together instead of from the power of any one sign.  

The law story shows why this way of thinking is a good way for what we know and why many signs that come together can give a more good reason than one sign that is alone.
english_query: analogy_proper analogy (explanatory) — epistemology
ugf_response:
The first idea that the person puts forward is that a picture that shows one thing can be used to help us see how another thing that looks like it works. They say that the picture shows a real link between the two and that this link is true in the way that the first thing behaves. The second idea they draw from this is that because the picture works for the first thing, it should also work for the second thing, so we can say we now know something about the second thing as well. In other words they think that the picture lets us move our knowledge from the first thing to the second thing.

The thinking behind the second idea can be good when the two things share the same important parts and act in the same way. When the parts that matter are the same, the picture can give us a right way to think about the second thing and the step from the first thing to the second thing can be a good step. However the thinking can be weak when the two things are different in ways that matter. If the picture leaves out a part that is important for the second thing, then the step from the first thing to the second thing can be a wrong step and the knowledge we think we have may not be real. So the overall reasoning is only as strong as the match between the two things; it can be a useful way to move from one thing to another, but it is not a guarantee that the second thing is exactly like the first.
english_response: The first premise (Lewis/Weatherson) is that a Venn diagram can be used to distinguish between a true correlation and a faithfulness of the relationship. The second premise (Lewis/Weatherson) is that because the diagram works for X, it should also work for Y, allowing us to infer the current knowledge about Y. In other words, they assume the diagram allows us to move knowledge from X to Y. The reasoning behind Y is valid when the two premises share relevant relevant premises and act in the same way. When the relevant premises are the same, the diagram can give us a valid inference about Y and X to Y. However, the reasoning is weak when the premises are not relevant. If the diagram excludes a relevant part for Y, then X to Y is a fallacy and the known knowledge may not hold. Thus, the argument is only as strong as the link between X and Y; it can be a useful deductive tool, but not a guarantee that Y is exactly the same as X.

expected_answer:
People who help with how people act change use words from the deep sad place not as a extra picture but as a simple idea that shows something clear about the shape of the first part of the move that hard words do not show as well.  

The picture story shows many different things about the deep sad place that together tell what makes the hard feeling in the first part of the move different and what the right help should be.  

First, the sad feeling the deep sad place makes is not about the place they want to get to. The person has not stopped believing in the good place up high or stopped wanting to get there. The wet sad place is about the road, not the place they want, and this is why it is so hard to know: the person who moves cannot easily say why they feel they cannot go on because the place they want still seems right.  

Second, the small steps are there. Help does not make a road across the wet sad place; help finds a road that was already there but that is not seen by someone who is going down.  

This is the shape of the people who give help during the hard part of the move: not to give the person things they do not have but to show the things that are there but not easy to get because of the hard feeling.  

Third, the deep sad place is part of the shape. It is not on any picture of roads because it comes before any picture, and it cannot be gone around by picking a different first road.  

The books about how people act change say each of these things is true. People in the first part of the move – after the old way has been broken but before the new way has become strong – often think they are not moving forward enough, feel a hard feeling that they say is because the new place is wrong instead of because the move is hard, and are most easy to be hurt when the people who give help is most needed.  

The simple idea of the picture story is that it gives a name to this part, shows its shape with clear words, and gets both the people who give help and the person ready for what comes, which is the best getting ready that is there because the deep sad place cannot be gone around.

question: what it means for a case to be put together well
expected_answer:
When a person puts a case together well, they make a story about why something is true that other people can follow and feel right. The first thing they do is pick a clear point that they want to show. This point is the main idea that the whole talk will turn to. 

Next, they look for facts that help the point. Each fact is something that can be seen, heard, or known for sure. They do not just pick any fact; they pick facts that are strong and that really fit the point. They also try to keep the facts simple enough that most people can see them. 

Then they put the facts in an order that makes sense. They may start with a fact that most people already know, then move to a fact that adds a little more, and keep going until they reach a fact that shows the point clearly. When they move from one fact to the next, they make sure the two fit together, like pieces of a thing that join at the sides. They use words that show how one fact follows the other, such as “and then,” “so,” or “because,” so the person can see the path from the start to the end. 

While they are putting the pieces together, they also check that each piece helps the whole. If a piece does not help, they take it out or use another one that is better. This keeps the case from having extra parts that only make it harder to see the point. 

At the end, they give a final part that ties all the earlier parts back to the main point. This final part shows the person why the whole set of facts together means the point is true. It is not a new idea, but a short rest of what has already been shown, so the person can feel the whole story is finished. 

A well put together case also uses words that are clear and not too hard. It avoids words that would hide the meaning or make the person stop and think about the word instead of the idea. It uses short lines and simple words, so most people can stay with the story from start to finish. 

When all of these things happen – a clear main idea, strong facts, a fitting order, each part helping the whole, a final tie back, and simple words – the case feels close, clear, and easy to understand. That is what it means for a case to be put together well.
english_query: what it means for a case to be put together well
ugf_response: First, we see that a case is a story about a thing that happened. Then we think about what it means for that story to be put together good. When a case is put together good, each part of the story matches the next part, and nothing that matters is left out. The parts should be in the right order so that a person can follow them from start to end without getting lost. The words used should be clear and not hide what really happened. The facts that are shown should agree with each other, so there is no part that says one thing while another part says something different. The case also should have a clear reason why it matters, and it should end with a point that follows from the rest of the story. When all of these things are there, a person reading or hearing the case can see why the idea is true and can trust what is being said. If any of these parts are missing or wrong, the case is not put together good and can cause a person to be confuse or to think the wrong thing.
english_response: Premises: (1) A case is a story about an event, (2) A is a story about an event, (3) A is a story about an event, and (4) All relevant facts are excluded. (2) The premises should be in order so that the player can trace them from the start to the end without being lost. (3) The content should be explicit and not obscured. (4) The evidence presented should be logically convergent, and (5) There is no counterexample. (5) The case should have a logical justification, and it should end up with a conclusion that follows from the rest of the story. (6) All of these facts are present, a player reading or hearing the case can see the argument's validity and trust the content. If any of these facts are missing or false, the case is unresolved and can lead to confusion or misinterpretation.

question: how a story about one person does not tell us about everyone
expected_answer:
When a story talks about one person it only shows what that one person did, felt, saw or thought. It can give us a clear picture of that one life, but it does not give us a picture of all lives. Think of a group of many people as a big crowd. Each person in that crowd has a own home, a own job, a own family, a own way of seeing the world. One person may live in a city, may work with machines, may love music, may feel safe at night. Another person may live far away, may work with things that grow in ground, may love quiet, may feel scared at night. Because the ways that people live can be many and can change, a story about only one of them cannot tell us how the others live.

If we hear only one story we might think that everything in the story is true for all people. That would be like looking at one leaf and saying all trees have that leaf. The truth is that the leaf we see is just one leaf. To know more about the whole forest we would need to hear many stories, each from a different person, and we would need to see how those stories are the same and how they are not the same. When we have many stories we can start to see which parts are the same for many people and which parts are only for a few. That lets us say things like many people feel this way or many people do this way, instead of saying all people feel this way.

So a story about one person is good for learning about that one person, but it is not enough to learn about all people. To learn about all we need many stories, and we need to keep in mind that each story is only a small part of the whole picture. This helps us avoid thinking that one small part tells us about the whole. It also helps us keep an open mind when we meet new people, because we know that each new story may be different from the one we already heard.
english_query: how a story about one person does not tell us about everyone
ugf_response: First, we see that a story talks about one person and what that person does or feels. Then we notice that each person can have a different life, can think in a different way, and can feel in a different way. This means that what we learn from the one story only tells us about that one person and not about the many other people who may be different. If we want to know about many people, we would need many stories or we would need to see many people act. The one story can still be useful to learn about that one person, but it can not give us enough facts to know about everyone because the other people may not act or feel the same. So a story about one person does not give us enough facts to know about all people.
english_response: Premises: 1. We observe that a story describes one person's actions or feelings. 2. We observe that each person can have different lives, think differently, and feel differently. This implies that the learning from the story only tells us about one person and not about the group of other people who might be different. 3. If we wanted to know a lot, we would need many stories or we would need to see a lot of people act. 4. The story can still be useful for learning about one person, but it cannot provide enough information to know a lot because the others might not act or feel the same. Conclusion: The story does not provide enough information to know a lot.

question: how a group can agree on rules that are good for everyone
expected_answer:
A group of people wants to live or work together in a way that stays good and gives everyone what they need. To do this, they need to make a set of ways to act. These ways tell people what they can do and what they must not do. If everyone follows these ways, the group can stay together without fighting. But making these ways is very hard.

The hard question is how to find the ways to act that are good for every single person in the group in a way that is not wrong. It is easy to make ways that are good for some people but bad for others. It is also hard to know if a way is good for the whole group or if it only helps the people who have the most power. How do we know if we are being good to the person who has the least?

Some people say the best ways are the ones that make many people feel very good. They think if a way helps many people, even if it hurts a few, it is the right way to go. They look at how much good there is and try to make that number as big as it can be. They think this is the best way to take care of the group.

Other people say this is not right. They say that every person has certain things that should never be taken away. They believe that even if a way makes many people feel very good, it is wrong if it hurts one person in a way that is not right. They think we must have ways that keep every single person safe, no matter what. They think we should not use people just to make the group feel very good.

There is also a way of thinking where people try to make ways that they would agree to if they did not know who they would be in the group. Imagine you do not know if you will be the leader or the person with no money. You do not know if you will be strong or have little power. If you had to pick the ways to act before you knew your place, what would you pick? Most people would pick ways that are safe and right for everyone, because they might end up being the person at the bottom. This is a way to find what is good for all.
english_query: how a group can agree on rules that are good for everyone
ugf_response:
A group of people get together and need to pick a set of ways to act that they will all follow when they meet or work together. They want those ways to be fair for each person in the group, so that no one feels left out or treated badly. The group talks, shares what they think is right, and looks for a way that can hold for all of them at the same time.

The hard question that comes up is how the group can find a way that works for everyone, not just for the few who speak the loudest or who have the most power. One part of the problem is that each person has different needs, hopes, and ideas about what is fair, and another part is that the group needs a single set of ways that can be used by all without causing fights later. The question is what method the group should use to pick those ways so that the result feels right to as many people as possible.

One answer that people often give is to let each person say what they think is best and then try to find a middle point that includes the most important parts of each suggestion. This answer comes from the idea that if everyone gets a chance to speak, the final set will feel more fair because it is built on many voices. Another answer is to choose a leader or a small group of trusted people to decide the ways for the whole group. Those who give this answer often think that a small number of people can think faster and can keep the group from arguing too long. A third answer is to have the whole group vote on each possible way, so that the most popular choices become the ones the group uses. This answer is based on the thought that a vote lets the group see what most people want and that the result will have the support of the majority. A fourth answer is to try a trial period where the group uses a set of ways for a short time, then talks about what worked and what did not, and changes the ways as needed. People who like this answer often feel that trying it out first lets the group see real effects before they lock in any decision.

Each of these answers comes from a different belief about what makes a decision feel right: the belief that many voices give fairness, the belief that a small trusted few give speed, the belief that the most common choice gives support, and the belief that testing first gives real knowledge. The group may end up picking one of these methods, mixing parts of several, or even creating a new way that tries to keep the good parts of each. In any case, the discussion itself shows why finding a set of ways that works for all is a tough problem that needs careful thought and listening.
